generatedSentence: Dr. Aris Thorne, a self-imposed ronin, lived a life of deliberate moderation in a remote coastal town. Her once balanced existence felt increasingly unbalanced as winter set in. A strange prediction of doomsday had appeared in her encrypted inbox, an astonishing claim that threatened to shatter the finality of her quiet retirement. She often pondered the deprivation such an event would bring, using an old utensil to stir her hazelnut coffee. A small whale carcass had been found near the docking station, an unusual sight. She considered her archery skills, still sharp and available, as a former operative. The anonymous sender made a cryptic wisecrack, suggesting they knew her darling secrets. The sheer energy of the threat seemed to fabricate a new reality, leaving her to wonder if this was merely a pedestrian hoax or something far more sinister. The sender, a notorious figure, seemed intent to undermine her resolve with a confusing narrative that would terrify any ordinary citizen. She followed a trail through the desolate junkyard on the edge of the county, observing the odd exhibition of salvaged parts. Her investigation required navigating complex jurisprudence, as the culprit’s identity continued to mystify her. An unspoken tension built, picking up tempo like a frantic cartoon chase. She remembered a former partner, a graduate of her academy who once adopted a grinch-like persona, now possibly related to this bizarre gimmick. The clues were like a half-eaten enchilada, messy and hard to decipher, making her wish for a painkiller for the intellectual strain. She realized this was no jovial prank, but a dangerous game, wandering across strange geography to find the unicum behind it all. The signal led Aris to a forgotten bunker, hidden beneath a field of dying sunflowers, emitting a strange noise. Inside, a short, jumbo-sized adult man, a self-proclaimed 'digital munchkin,' was juggling satellite feeds, his vitality astonishing. He was a technological prodigy who had been interned years ago, now enacting a whimsical embargo on global data, believing it was time to recycle society. Holographic whales swam across the screens, a gorgeous and resplendent display of his warped vision. He spoke of an important emergency, the need to hydrate the collective consciousness. Aris noticed he was wearing a laced scarf, probably knitting related, a hint of domesticity amidst the cosmic chaos. This rogue gamekeeper of information, oblivious to the impending global panic, offered her a cold beer while playing ethereal music on invisible flutes, speaking of longitude and the end of the digital age. Aris knew this haughty prodigy had made a grave mistake. She moved with purpose, quickly shutting a critical valve, severing the data torrent and ushered in a new era of digital security. The screens, which had so vividly displayed virtual whales, went dark behind the grimy windows of the bunker. The 'Munchkin' was no winner this time. She knew she didn't deserve praise, yet felt a quiet satisfaction. The system, once rampant, now needed a new navigator to learn its intricacies. She imagined him trying to bargain his way out, but his grand scheme, a sort of digital voodoo intended to be a global matchmaker of information, had failed. It was no toy or trilogy of harmless events. An apple sat on a desk, a stark contrast to the complex machinery. Outside, a single okapi grazed a kilometer away, its hide patterned like ancient lichen, a silent witness to the boing of silence that followed the sticky confrontation. She wouldn't dance with fate; she controlled it.

A strange prediction of doomsday had appeared in her encrypted inbox, an astonishing claim that threatened to shatter the finality of her quiet retirement. She often pondered the deprivation such an event would bring, using an old utensil to stir her hazelnut coffee. A small whale carcass had been found near the docking station, an unusual sight. She considered her archery skills, still sharp and available, as a former operative. The anonymous sender made a cryptic wisecrack, suggesting they knew her darling secrets. The sheer energy of the threat seemed to fabricate a new reality, leaving her to wonder if this was merely a pedestrian hoax or something far more sinister.

The sender, a notorious figure, seemed intent to undermine her resolve with a confusing narrative that would terrify any ordinary citizen. She followed a trail through the desolate junkyard on the edge of the county, observing the odd exhibition of salvaged parts. Her investigation required navigating complex jurisprudence, as the culprit’s identity continued to mystify her. An unspoken tension built, picking up tempo like a frantic cartoon chase. She remembered a former partner, a graduate of her academy who once adopted a grinch-like persona, now possibly related to this bizarre gimmick. The clues were like a half-eaten enchilada, messy and hard to decipher, making her wish for a painkiller for the intellectual strain. She realized this was no jovial prank, but a dangerous game, wandering across strange geography to find the unicum behind it all.

The signal led Aris to a forgotten bunker, hidden beneath a field of dying sunflowers, emitting a strange noise. Inside, a short, jumbo-sized adult man, a self-proclaimed 'digital munchkin,' was juggling satellite feeds, his vitality astonishing. He was a technological prodigy who had been interned years ago, now enacting a whimsical embargo on global data, believing it was time to recycle society. Holographic whales swam across the screens, a gorgeous and resplendent display of his warped vision. He spoke of an important emergency, the need to hydrate the collective consciousness. Aris noticed he was wearing a laced scarf, probably knitting related, a hint of domesticity amidst the cosmic chaos. This rogue gamekeeper of information, oblivious to the impending global panic, offered her a cold beer while playing ethereal music on invisible flutes, speaking of longitude and the end of the digital age.

Aris knew this haughty prodigy had made a grave mistake. She moved with purpose, quickly shutting a critical valve, severing the data torrent and ushered in a new era of digital security. The screens, which had so vividly displayed virtual whales, went dark behind the grimy windows of the bunker. The 'Munchkin' was no winner this time. She knew she didn't deserve praise, yet felt a quiet satisfaction. The system, once rampant, now needed a new navigator to learn its intricacies. She imagined him trying to bargain his way out, but his grand scheme, a sort of digital voodoo intended to be a global matchmaker of information, had failed. It was no toy or trilogy of harmless events. An apple sat on a desk, a stark contrast to the complex machinery. Outside, a single okapi grazed a kilometer away, its hide patterned like ancient lichen, a silent witness to the boing of silence that followed the sticky confrontation. She wouldn't dance with fate; she controlled it.
rawPanelSentences: Dr. Aris Thorne, a self-imposed **ronin**, lived a life of deliberate **moderation** in a remote coastal town. Her once **balanced** existence felt increasingly **unbalanced** as **winter** set in. A strange **prediction** of **doomsday** had appeared in her encrypted **inbox**, an **astonishing** claim that threatened to shatter the **finality** of her quiet retirement. She often pondered the **deprivation** such an event would bring, using an old **utensil** to stir her **hazelnut** coffee. A small **whale** carcass had been found near the **docking** station, an unusual sight. She considered her **archery** skills, still sharp and **available**, as a former operative. The anonymous sender made a cryptic **wisecrack**, suggesting they knew her **darling** secrets. The sheer **energy** of the threat seemed to **fabricate** a new reality, leaving her to wonder if this was merely a **pedestrian** hoax or something far more sinister.

The sender, a **notorious** figure, seemed intent to **undermine** her resolve with a confusing **narrative** that would **terrify** any ordinary citizen. She followed a trail through the desolate **junkyard** on the edge of the **county**, observing the odd **exhibition** of salvaged parts. Her investigation required navigating complex **jurisprudence**, as the culprit’s identity continued to **mystify** her. An **unspoken** tension built, picking up **tempo** like a frantic **cartoon** chase. She remembered a former partner, a **graduate** of her academy who once adopted a **grinch**-like persona, now possibly **related** to this bizarre **gimmick**. The clues were like a half-eaten **enchilada**, messy and hard to decipher, making her wish for a **painkiller** for the intellectual strain. She realized this was no **jovial** prank, but a dangerous game, **wandering** across strange **geography** to find the **unicum** behind it all.

The signal led Aris to a forgotten bunker, hidden beneath a field of dying **sunflower**s, emitting a strange **noise**. Inside, a short, **jumbo**-sized adult man, a self-proclaimed 'digital **munchkin**,' was **juggling** satellite feeds, his **vitality** astonishing. He was a technological **prodigy** who had been **interned** years ago, now enacting a **whimsical** **embargo** on global data, believing it was time to **recycle** society. Holographic **whales** swam across the screens, a **gorgeous** and **resplendent** display of his warped vision. He spoke of an **important** **emergency**, the need to **hydrate** the collective consciousness. Aris noticed he was wearing a **laced** scarf, probably **knitting** related, a hint of domesticity amidst the cosmic chaos. This rogue **gamekeeper** of information, oblivious to the impending global panic, offered her a cold **beer** while playing ethereal music on invisible **flutes**, speaking of **longitude** and the end of the digital age.

Aris knew this **haughty** **prodigy** had made a grave **mistake**. She moved with purpose, quickly shutting a critical **valve**, severing the data **torrent** and **ushered** in a new era of digital security. The screens, which had so **vividly** displayed virtual **whales**, went dark behind the grimy **windows** of the bunker. The 'Munchkin' was no **winner** this time. She knew she didn't **deserve** praise, yet felt a quiet satisfaction. The system, once **rampant**, now needed a new **navigator** to **learn** its intricacies. She imagined him trying to **bargain** his way out, but his grand scheme, a sort of digital **voodoo** intended to be a global **matchmaker** of information, had failed. It was no **toy** or **trilogy** of harmless events. An **apple** sat on a desk, a stark contrast to the complex machinery. Outside, a single **okapi** grazed a **kilometer** away, its hide patterned like ancient **lichen**, a silent witness to the **boing** of silence that followed the **sticky** confrontation. She wouldn't **dance** with fate; she controlled it.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, neo-noir cyber-thriller style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist: a grizzled adult woman, Dr. Aris Thorne, with sharp, intelligent eyes, prominent facial wrinkles, and short, practical graying hair. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Aris Thorne sips coffee in a dimly lit, cozy cabin, looking intently at a glowing tablet screen, establishing shot from a medium angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Dr. Aris Thorne navigates a sprawling, derelict junkyard filled with electronic waste, a wide shot from a high angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Dr. Aris Thorne confronts a short, stocky adult man with a receding hairline and intense focus, surrounded by blinking server racks and holographic whale projections, a close-up on their faces from a low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Dr. Aris Thorne stands silhouetted against a dusty bunker window, observing an okapi grazing peacefully in the distance, a medium shot from a slightly high angle.

definitions.definition: A walker; one who walks or goes on foot, especially as opposed to one who uses a vehicle.
Specifically, an expert or professional walker or runner; one who performs feats of walking or running.
Of or intended for those who are walking.
definitions.example: pedestrian crossing

definitions.definition: Ordinary, dull; everyday; unexceptional.
definitions.example: His manner of dress was pedestrian but tidy.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to ordinary, everyday movements incorporated in postmodern dance.
definitions.example: The choreographer prefers pedestrian movements.

definitions.definition: A statement of what will happen in the future.
A probability estimation based on statistical methods.
The act of depriving, dispossessing, or bereaving; the act of deposing or divesting of some dignity.
The state of being deprived
The taking away from a clergyman of his benefice, or other spiritual promotion or dignity.
(followed by “of”) lack
definitions.example: He was suffering from deprivation of sleep.

definitions.definition: To cut off a section of an animal's tail, to practise a caudectomy.
To reduce (wages); to deduct from.
To cut off, bar, or destroy.
definitions.example: to dock an entail

definitions.definition: To land at a harbour.
To join two moving items.
To drag a user interface element (such as a toolbar) to a position on screen where it snaps into place.
To place (an electronic device) in its dock.
definitions.example: I docked the laptop and allowed it to recharge for an hour.

definitions.definition: To pierce with holes, as pricking pastry or dough with a fork to prevent excessive rising in the oven.
The process of cutting off or trimming the tail or ears of an animal.
The securing of a vessel to the quayside with cables
The process of connecting one spacecraft to another.
The sex act involving two men co-joined by their penises, with overlapping foreskins, coupling them together by their penises.
A method which predicts the preferred orientation of one molecule to a second when bound to each other to form a stable complex.
A person who is dear to one.
definitions.example: Mary, the youngest daughter, was always her mother's darling.

definitions.definition: A kind or sweet person; sweetheart.
definitions.example: The girl next door picks up all my shopping for me. She is such a darling.

definitions.definition: An affectionate term of address.
definitions.example: Pass the wine, would you darling?

definitions.definition: (by extension) A person or thing very popular with a certain group.
definitions.example: a darling of the theatre

definitions.definition: Dear; cherished.
definitions.example: She is my darling wife of twenty-two years.

definitions.definition: Charming
definitions.example: Well isn't that a darling little outfit she has on.

definitions.definition: The day when God is expected to judge the world; end times.
Judgement day; the day of the Final Judgment; any day of decisive judgement or final dissolution.
Concerned with or predicting future universal destruction.
Given to or marked by forebodings or predictions of impending calamity.
Capable of causing widespread or total destruction.
A masterless samurai (who often becomes a mercenary to make ends meet).

(in Japan) A student who has failed the entrance examination for the high school or university of their choice: if the student does not wish to take the exam the following year or is unable to, the student becomes a ronin, assuming full responsibility for their own training and survival.
To bring (items) to an equipoise, as the scales of a balance by adjusting the weights.
To make (concepts) agree.
To hold (an object or objects) precariously; to support on a narrow base, so as to keep from falling.
definitions.example: I balanced my mug of coffee on my knee.

definitions.definition: To compare in relative force, importance, value, etc.; to estimate.
(dancing) To move toward, and then back from, reciprocally.
definitions.example: to balance partners

definitions.definition: To contract, as a sail, into a narrower compass.
definitions.example: to balance the boom mainsail

definitions.definition: To make the credits and debits of (an account) correspond.
definitions.example: This final payment, or credit, balances the account.

definitions.definition: To be in equilibrium.
To have matching credits and debits.
To weigh in a balance.
To hesitate or fluctuate.
Containing elements in appropriate proportion; proportionately weighted on all dimensions and therefore unlikely to tip over.
definitions.example: He believed he rarely got sick because of his balanced diet.

definitions.definition: The state or quality of being moderate; avoidance of extremes
An instance of moderating: bringing something away from extremes, especially in a beneficial way
The process of moderating a discussion
definitions.example: The moderation of a large online forum can be hard work.

definitions.definition: To cause to be out of balance.
definitions.example: If you put that weight on the edge of the tray, it will unbalance it and dump all of the dishes on the floor.

definitions.definition: Not balanced, without equilibrium; dizzy
Irrational or mentally deranged
Not adjusted such that debit and credit correspond
Of an expression having different numbers of left and right parentheses
An offensive line with more players on one side of the center than on the other
An instrument or device for domestic use, especially in the kitchen.
definitions.example: We have convenient storage for all the kitchen utensils.

definitions.definition: A useful small tool, implement, or vessel.
definitions.example: He stocked up on old-style writing utensils.

definitions.definition: The act of entering.
Permission to enter.
definitions.example: Children are allowed entry only if accompanied by an adult.

definitions.definition: A doorway that provides a means of entering a building.
The act of taking possession.
The start of an insurance contract.
A passageway between terraced houses that provides a means of entering a back garden or yard.
A small room immediately inside the front door of a house or other building, often having an access to a stairway and leading on to other rooms
A small group formed within a church, especially Episcopal, for simple dinner and fellowship, and to help facilitate new friendships
An item in a list, such as an article in a dictionary or encyclopedia.
A record made in a log, diary or anything similarly organized; a datum in a database.
definitions.example: What does the entry for 2 August 2005 say?

definitions.definition: A term at any position in a matrix.
definitions.example: The entry in the second row and first column of this matrix is 6.

definitions.definition: The exhibition or depositing of a ship's papers at the customhouse, to procure licence to land goods; or the giving an account of a ship's cargo to the officer of the customs, and obtaining his permission to land the goods.
The point when a musician starts to play or sing; entrance.
The impetus behind all motion and all activity.
The capacity to do work.
A quantity that denotes the ability to do work and is measured in a unit dimensioned in mass × distance²/time² (ML²/T²) or the equivalent.

An intangible, modifiable force (often characterized as either 'positive' or 'negative') believed in some New Age religions to emanate from a person, place or thing and which is (or can be) preserved and transferred in human interactions; shared mood or group habit; a vibe, a feeling, an impression. (Compare aura.)

(often plural) The external actions and influences resulting from an entity’s internal nature (ousia) and by which it is made manifest, as opposed to that internal nature itself; the aspect of an entity that can affect the wider world and be apprehended by other beings.
A measure of how many actions a player or unit can take; in the fantasy genre often called magic points or mana.
To form into a whole by uniting its parts; to construct; to build.
definitions.example: to fabricate a bridge or ship

definitions.definition: To form by art and labor; to manufacture; to produce.
definitions.example: to fabricate computer chips

definitions.definition: To invent and form; to forge; to devise falsely.
definitions.example: to fabricate a lie or story

definitions.definition: To cut up an animal as preparation for cooking, particularly used in reference to fowl.
The state of being final; the condition from which no further changes occur.
definitions.example: The finality of my father's death suddenly hit me: there would be no more bedtime stories, no more games of catch in the back yard.

definitions.definition: Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
Someone with dark skin, eyes and hair, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
An appliance to be fixed on the front of a grate, to keep a kettle warm, etc.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).
definitions.example: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.

definitions.definition: To store something (for instance animals) somewhere over winter to protect it from cold.
A witty or sarcastic comment or quip.
To make a sarcastic, flippant, or sardonic comment.
A small door or gate, especially one beside a larger one.
A small window or other opening, sometimes fitted with a grating.
A service window, as in a bank or train station, where a customer conducts transactions with a teller; a ticket barrier at a rail station, box office at a cinema, etc.
One of the two wooden structures at each end of the pitch, consisting of three vertical stumps and two bails; the target for the bowler, defended by the batsman.
A dismissal; the act of a batsman getting out.
The period during which two batsmen bat together.
The pitch.
The area around the stumps where the batsmen stand.
Any of the small arches through which the balls are driven.
A temporary metal attachment that one attaches one's lift-ticket to.
A shelter made from tree boughs, used by lumbermen.
The space between the pillars, in post-and-stall working.
An angle bracket when used in HTML.
Any of several species of large sea mammals of the infraorder Cetacea.
Something, or someone, that is very large.
Something, or someone, that is excellent.
In a casino, a person who routinely bets at the maximum limit allowable.
An investor who deals with very large amounts of money.
(by extension) A video game player who spends large amounts of money on premium content.
To hunt for whales.
Any animal belonging to the Suidae family of mammals, especially the pig, the warthog, and the boar.
(specifically) An adult swine (contrasted with a pig, a young swine).
A greedy person; one who refuses to share.
A large motorcycle, particularly a Harley-Davidson.
A young sheep that has not been shorn.
A rough, flat scrubbing broom for scrubbing a ship's bottom under water.
A device for mixing and stirring the pulp from which paper is made.
A shilling coin; its value, 12 old pence.
A tanner, a sixpence coin; its value.
A half-crown coin; its value, 30 old pence.
The effect of the middle of the hull of a ship rising while the ends droop
To greedily take more than one's share, to take precedence at the expense of another or others.
definitions.example: Hey! Quit hogging all the blankets.

definitions.definition: To clip the mane of a horse, making it short and bristly.
To scrub with a hog, or scrubbing broom.
To cause the keel of a ship to arch upwards (the opposite of sag).
The fruit of the hazel tree.
A container in which papers to be dealt with are put.
An electronic folder serving the same purpose.
The aggregate of items that demand one's attention or effort.
definitions.example: The kids, my ex, my parents, the job, bills — my inbox is full.

definitions.definition: To put (something) in someone's inbox.
definitions.example: I just inboxed you the presentation.

definitions.definition: To communicate with (a person) by writing to their electronic inbox.
Such as one may avail oneself of; capable of being used for the accomplishment of a purpose.
definitions.example: We have an available candidate.

definitions.definition: Readily obtainable.
definitions.example: The list shows the available products in the store.

definitions.definition: Valid.
definitions.example: This is an available plea.

definitions.definition: Having sufficient power, force, or efficacy to achieve the purpose; availing, effective.
Free to meet someone, speak on the telephone, enter a romantic relationship or the like.
definitions.example: Hi, this is Mark Smith calling. Is your mother available?

definitions.definition: To surprise greatly.
Causing astonishment.
The practice or sport of shooting arrows with a bow.
A group of archers.
Widely known, especially for something negative; infamous.
The systematic recitation of an event or series of events.
That which is narrated.
A representation of an event or story.
(creative writing) A manner of conveying a story, fictional or otherwise, in a body of work.
definitions.example: The plot is full of holes, but the narrative is extremely compelling.

definitions.definition: Telling a story.
Overly talkative; garrulous.
Of or relating to narration.
definitions.example: the narrative thrust of a film

definitions.definition: A specimen of the now extinct species Homo neanderthalensis.
A primitive person.
Of or pertaining to Homo neanderthalensis.
definitions.example: The capacity of the Neanderthal skull was 10% larger than that of modern humans.

definitions.definition: Old-fashioned, opposed to change (in allusion to Homo neanderthalensis).
Of or pertaining to the Neander Valley in Germany.
A drug that numbs the pain in the body.
The theoretical study of law.
Cheerful and good-humoured; jolly, merry.
Pertaining to the astrological influence of the planet Jupiter; having the characteristics of a person under such influence (see sense 1).
A place where rubbish is placed.
A business that sells used metal or items.
To tell in a descriptive way.
definitions.example: Please relate the circumstances of your journey here today.

definitions.definition: To bring into a relation, association, or connection (between one thing and another).
To have a connection.
definitions.example: The patterns on the screen relate to the pitch and volume of the music being played.

definitions.definition: To interact.
To respond through reaction.
(with to) To identify with; to understand.
definitions.example: I find it difficult to relate to others because I'm extremely introverted.

definitions.definition: To bring back; to restore.
Standing in relation or connection.
definitions.example: Electric and magnetic forces are closely related.

definitions.definition: Being a relative of.
definitions.example: Everyone is related to their parents.

definitions.definition: Narrated; told.
Fulfilling a relation.
(in combination) Having a relationship with the thing named
definitions.example: Gun-related crime.

definitions.definition: Connected to or depending on something else; comparative.
(of a URL, URI, path, or similar) Expressed in relation to another item, rather than in complete form.
definitions.example: The relative URL /images/pic.jpg, when evaluated in the context of http://example.com/docs/pic.html, corresponds to the absolute URL http://example.com/images/pic.jpg.

definitions.definition: (grammar) That relates to an antecedent.
Having the same key but differing in being major or minor.
Relevant; pertinent; related.
definitions.example: relative to your earlier point about taxes, ...

definitions.definition: Capable to be changed by other beings or circumstance; conditional.
To thoroughly confuse, befuddle, or bewilder.
definitions.example: Solar eclipses continued to mystify ancient humans for thousands of years.

definitions.definition: To dig underneath (something), to make a passage for destructive or military purposes; to sap.
To weaken or work against; to hinder, sabotage.
definitions.example: The war efforts were undermined by the constant bickering between the allies.

definitions.definition: To erode the base or foundation of something, e.g. by the action of water.
A unique example or specimen of something
To retract what one has spoken, to unsay.
(sometimes postpositive) Not spoken; not said.
(sometimes postpositive) Not formally articulated or stated; implicit or understood.
definitions.example: The unspoken rule is to start a new pot of coffee when it is empty.

definitions.definition: An instance of exhibiting, or something exhibited.
A large-scale public showing of objects or products.
definitions.example: There was an art exhibition on in the town hall.

definitions.definition: A financial award or prize given to a student (who becomes an exhibitioner) by a school or university, usually on the basis of academic merit.
A game which does not impact the standings for any major cup or competition.
A Mexican dish made by wrapping a filling in a tortilla, then baking in a sauce.
To move without purpose or specified destination; often in search of livelihood.
definitions.example: to wander over the fields

definitions.definition: To stray; stray from one's course; err.
definitions.example: A writer wanders from his subject.

definitions.definition: To commit adultery.
To go somewhere indirectly or at varying speeds; to move in a curved path.
Of the mind, to lose focus or clarity of argument or attention.
Travelling with no preset route; roaming.
Irregular turning of the eyes.
Aimless thought.
Straying from a desired path.
(chiefly in the plural) Disordered speech or delirium.
Which wanders; travelling from place to place.
(of an organ) Abnormally capable of moving in certain directions.
definitions.example: a wandering kidney; a wandering liver

definitions.definition: A humorous drawing, often with a caption, or a strip of such drawings.
A drawing satirising current public figures.
An artist's preliminary sketch.
An animated piece of film which is often but not exclusively humorous.
A diagram in a scientific concept.
To draw a cartoon, a humorous drawing.
To make a preliminary sketch.
The land ruled by a count or a countess.

An administrative region of various countries, including Bhutan, Canada, China, Croatia, Ireland, Japan, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Romania, South Korea, Sweden, the United Kingdom, and 48 of the 50 United States (excluding Alaska and Louisiana).
A definitive geographic region, without direct administrative functions.
A jail operated by a county government.
definitions.example: He can't come; he's up in the county for agg assault.

definitions.definition: Characteristic of a ‘county family’; representative of the gentry or aristocracy of a county.
The act of commemorating; an observance or celebration to honor the memory of some person or event.
That which serves the purpose of commemorating; a memorial.
The specification of individual saints in the prayers for the dead; the great festival of the Oxford academic year, usually taking place on the third Wednesday after Trinity Sunday.
To frighten greatly; to fill with terror.
To menace or intimidate.
To make terrible.
A frequency or rate.

A move which is part of one's own plan or strategy and forces, e.g. by means of a check or attacking a piece, the opponent to make a move which is not bad but of no use for him (the player gains a tempo, the opponent loses a tempo), or equivalently a player achieves the same result in fewer moves by one approach rather than another.
The timing advantage of being on lead, thus being first to initiate a strategy to develop tricks for one's side.
The timing of a particular event – earlier or later than in an alternative situation (as in chess example)
The number of beats per minute in a piece of music; also, an indicative term denoting approximate rate of speed in written music (examples: allegro, andante)
The steady pace set by the frontmost riders.

A small truck or cargo van with three or four wheels, commonly used for commercial transport and deliveries (particularly in Asian and African countries): a genericized trademark, originally associated with the manufacturer Vidal & Sohn Tempo-Werke GmbH.
A rapid rate of play by the offense resulting from reducing the amount of time which elapses after one play ends and the next starts.
A description of the earth: a treatise or textbook on geography; an atlas or gazetteer.
The study of the physical properties of the earth, including how humans affect and are affected by them.

A territory: a geographical area as a field of business or market sector.
A person who is recognized by a university as having completed the requirements of a degree studied at the institution.

A person who is recognized as having completed any level of education.

definitions.definition: To mark (something) with degrees; to divide into regular steps or intervals, as the scale of a thermometer, a scheme of punishment or rewards, etc.
To change gradually.

definitions.definition: To prepare gradually; to arrange, temper, or modify by degrees or to a certain degree; to determine the degrees of.

Holding an academic degree

definitions.definition: A gimmick capacitor.
A night out with one's friends.

definitions.definition: To shed light upon.
To clarify something by giving, or serving as, an example or a comparison.

Shiny and colourful, and thus pleasing to the eye.
Exhibiting the property of resplendency in Peano arithmetic.

definitions.definition: One who is or exists.
The capacity to live and develop.
Energy or vigour.
That which distinguishes living from nonliving things; life, animateness.

Given to whimsy.

To thrash, to flog, to beat vigorously or soundly.
